yasbeIsACanadaBasedPaymentFintechForSmal: YASBe is a Canada-based payment fintech for small and medium businesses (SMBs). We provide an API-native Apus Payment Platform to manage SMBs' pay-in and pay-out in one working station, eliminating cross-border payment bottlenecks and frictions, reducing payment costs, and enhancing capital efficiency for SMBs.
ourGoalIsToConnectSmallAndMediumBusiness: Our goal is to connect small and medium businesses with the world economy. With Apus Payment Platform, we empower SMBs to expand their businesses internationally.
theComplianceDepartmentPlaysACrucialRole: The Compliance Department plays a crucial role in ensuring that Apus' products and processes align with regulatory standards. The team possesses exceptional problem-solving skills and excels in conducting tests within a fully digital environment.
weAreLookingForAPaymentsComplianceManage: We are looking for a Payments Compliance Manager who possesses a clear understanding of the measures required to ensure our consumer protection practice remains in compliance. If you are excited about establishing a Compliance Department from scratch and working with innovative product offerings, you will realize your biggest potential in this role. In more specific terms, we require an individual who will assume the responsibility of providing guidance to our business regarding compliance requirements and the necessary controls. It's important to note that this role is highly practical and hands-on in nature.
developImplementAndExecuteAMLCTFPolicies: Develop, implement, and execute AML/CTF policies and rulebooks/handbooks, to ensure that the company conducts its business in a regulatory-compliant environment
ensureEffectiveImplementationOfNewOrChan: Ensure effective implementation of new or changing Compliance/AML/CTF requirements initiated by the regulatory (e.g., FINTRAC, Bank of Canada, etc.), or as required by legislation or regulators by liaising and coordinating efforts with internal departments
maintainAndMonitorTheEffectivenessOfTheC: Maintain and monitor the effectiveness of the company's Compliance/AML programs and standards by developing and implementing compliance testing programs, reviewing internal controls to determine areas of improvement, identifying gaps, and ensuring that these are properly addressed
manageTheOperationOfAMLRegulatoryComplia: Manage the operation of AML Regulatory Compliance Management Framework (RCM) and maintain the regulatory/guidance inventory up to date. Identifying/maintaining up-to-date reference and source materials for AML legislation and ensuring access to relevant lines of business

developAndMaintainAMLATFTrainingMaterial: Develop and maintain AML/ATF training material and resources. Working with the HR department to develop effective compliance training programs, including appropriate compliance training material for new employees and ongoing training for all employees, managers, and the board of directors
